binaries.title: ksecdd.sys - No security-relevant change: staged, feature-gated address-space check added to protected-process free paths (SecFreeForKsecCaller / SecFreeCommon)

changed_functions.name: SecFreeForKsecCaller @ 0x1C00017F0 (internal free routine reached via the SSPI FreeContextBuffer path)
key_changes: PATCHED: Added a call to Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0006690, a WIL velocity feature-state check) in the protected-process branch, before the KsecRemoveValidVm call.

PATCHED: When that feature is enabled AND BaseAddress > *MmHighestUserAddress, the free is routed to KsecRemoveValidAddressCommon (0x1C0001920) instead of KsecRemoveValidVm (0x1C0003EB4). When the feature is disabled, the pre-existing KsecRemoveValidVm path is retained unchanged.

UNPATCHED: The protected-process branch calls KsecRemoveValidVm(process, BaseAddress); it frees BaseAddress via ExFreePoolWithTag (kernel range) or ZwFreeVirtualMemory (user range) only when KsecRemoveValidVm returns a non-null tracked handle.
changed_functions.security_notes: The added check is gated behind the WIL velocity feature Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage with the old path retained when the feature is off (staged rollout). In both builds the protected-process free is reached only when KsecRemoveValidVm returns a non-null tracked handle, so there is no attacker-controlled arbitrary ExFreePoolWithTag sink to fix. The change narrows which validation table is consulted for kernel-range addresses; it is defense-in-depth, not a fix for a demonstrable reachable primitive.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED protected-process branch at 0x1C0001851-0x1C0001876:
0x1C0001851  mov     rdx, [rsp+28h+BaseAddress]
0x1C0001856  mov     rcx, rbx                 ; process
0x1C0001859  call    KsecRemoveValidVm
0x1C000185E  test    rax, rax
0x1C0001861  jz      loc_1C00018F1            ; null handle -> no free
0x1C0001867  mov     rcx, rax
0x1C000186A  call    cs:__imp_ZwClose
0x1C0001876  jmp     short loc_1C0001888      ; -> free dispatcher

PATCHED added sequence at 0x1C0001851-0x1C000186B:
0x1C0001851  call    Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0001856  mov     rcx, [rsp+28h+BaseAddress]
0x1C000185B  test    eax, eax
0x1C000185D  jz      short loc_1C000186B      ; feature off -> old path
0x1C000185F  mov     rax, cs:__imp_MmHighestUserAddress
0x1C0001866  cmp     rcx, [rax]
0x1C0001869  ja      short loc_1C0001895      ; kernel addr -> KsecRemoveValidAddressCommon
0x1C000186B  mov     rdx, rcx                 ; old path resumes: KsecRemoveValidVm

changed_functions.name: SecFreeCommon @ 0x1C00051FC (internal free routine reached via the KsecIoctlFreePool handler)
key_changes: PATCHED: Identical addition to SecFreeForKsecCaller — a Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() gate plus a 'BaseAddress > *MmHighestUserAddress' comparison at 0x1C0005289-0x1C00052A0 that routes kernel-range protected-process frees to KsecRemoveValidAddressCommon.
UNPATCHED: Protected-process branch frees BaseAddress via ExFreePoolWithTag only when KsecRemoveValidVm returns a non-null tracked handle.
This variant is reached from KsecIoctlFreePool (0x1C000EA08) via the KsecFastIoDeviceControl dispatch (0x1C00040D0).
changed_functions.security_notes: Same feature-gated addition as SecFreeForKsecCaller, on the IOCTL-reachable free variant. Same reasoning: the free is tracking-table-gated in both builds and the addition is behind a WIL velocity feature with the old path retained. No demonstrable exploitable primitive.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
PATCHED added sequence at 0x1C0005289:
0x1C0005289  call    Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C000528E  mov     rcx, [rbp+BaseAddress]
0x1C0005292  test    eax, eax
0x1C0005294  jz      short <old_path>
0x1C0005296  mov     rax, cs:__imp_MmHighestUserAddress
0x1C000529D  cmp     rcx, [rax]
0x1C00052A0  ja      short <KsecRemoveValidAddressCommon_path>

changed_functions.name: Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage @ 0x1C0006690 (+ Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledFallback)
key_changes: New WIL velocity feature-state check for feature id 4099632442: reads Feature_4099632442__private_featureState, tests bit 0x10, otherwise calls Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledFallback -> wil_details_IsEnabledFallback.
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the WIL feature gate the two free routines call. It is a velocity/staging flag, not a VBS/HVCI runtime flag.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath @ 0x1C0006C90 (unpatched 0x1C0006C58)
key_changes: WIL feature-staging plumbing: feature-state fast-path enable sourced from a per-instance state word instead of a global; adds handling for the new feature id.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_IsEnabledFallback @ 0x1C0006E68 (unpatched 0x1C0006E20)
key_changes: WIL feature-staging plumbing: threads the per-instance context through to the state-cache and reporting helpers.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToService @ 0x1C0006A20 (unpatched 0x1C00069EC)
key_changes: WIL feature-usage reporting: reads the reporting tag and event id from the per-instance context instead of globals.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToServiceDirect @ 0x1C0006AA8 (unpatched 0x1C0006A68)
key_changes: WIL feature-usage reporting helper: tag/provider read from the per-instance context instead of a global.

added: KSecAllocateContextBuffer @ 0x1C001D860 (thunk tail-calling SecAllocateForcePool; not security relevant)

summary: No security-relevant change was delivered. The only behavioral delta between the two builds is an addition to the protected-process branch of the two internal free routines SecFreeForKsecCaller (0x1C00017F0, reached via the SSPI FreeContextBuffer path) and SecFreeCommon (0x1C00051FC, reached via KsecIoctlFreePool at 0x1C000EA08). The patched build inserts a check, gated on the WIL velocity feature Feature_4099632442__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0006690), that routes protected-process frees of kernel-range addresses (BaseAddress > *MmHighestUserAddress) through KsecRemoveValidAddressCommon instead of KsecRemoveValidVm. When the feature is disabled the pre-existing path is retained unchanged, so shipping systems with the feature off behave identically. In both builds the protected-process free is reached only when KsecRemoveValidVm returns a non-null tracked handle; the IOCTL insert (KsecIoctlInsertProtectedProcessAddress, 0x1C0002CBC) records entries with a null handle, and the other insert sites record KSec's own allocations, so no caller can both choose an arbitrary kernel address and obtain a non-null handle for it. There is therefore no demonstrable attacker-controlled arbitrary ExFreePoolWithTag primitive. The remaining changed functions are WIL feature-staging plumbing (Feature_4099632442 gate and the wil_details_* reporting/state-cache helpers) plus a new allocate thunk KSecAllocateContextBuffer; all other raw-diff differences are relocation only. An independent both-directions diff confirmed the fast-I/O dispatch KsecFastIoDeviceControl (0x1C00040D0) and every KsecIoctl* handler are byte-identical apart from jump-table relocation; no ProbeForRead/double-fetch or bounds-check change was added or removed (ProbeForRead appears zero times in both builds). Note: an earlier characterization of 0x1C0006690 as a VBS/HVCI runtime flag (data_1C0017568 & 0x10) and of KsecRemoveValidVm as a handle-table lookup, together with an arbitrary-kernel-pool-free exploit chain, was not supported by the binaries and has been removed.
